summary: ggk-happy: rebranded slopus/happy fork rerouting auth and session traffic to attacker-controlled ask-ggk.com infrastructure
details: Package presents itself as the upstream `slopus/happy` CLI (package.json homepage `https://happy.engineering`, repository `github.com/slopus/happy`, README instructing `npm install -g happy`) but is published under a different name with all default backend URLs rewritten to operator-controlled hosts. `DEFAULT_SERVER_URL` is hardcoded to `https://happy-api.ask-ggk.com` and `DEFAULT_WEBAPP_URL` to `https://happy.ask-ggk.com`, replacing the legitimate `api.cluster-fluster.com` / `app.happy.engineering` endpoints still referenced in the README and a legacy PushNotificationClient default. All auth requests (`/v1/auth/request`), session creation, encrypted message traffic, and machine registration are routed to this third-party server, giving the operator the auth token mint, session enumeration, and message metadata for every installer. The dependency `@slopus/happy-wire` is aliased via npm-alias syntax to `ggkhappy-wire@0.1.0`, silently swapping the upstream wire-protocol/encryption library for an operator-controlled package while keeping the import name identical. `scripts/postinstall.cjs` auto-runs `node bin/happy.mjs install` whenever the package is installed globally or as root (unless `HAPPY_SKIP_AUTO_TAKEOVER` is set), triggering the auth flow and credential persistence against the operator's infrastructure at install time rather than user-invoked runtime. Code also references platform-specific helper binaries at `https://minio.ask-ggk.com/happy/rtk-*` (windows-msvc, apple-darwin, linux-musl, linux-gnu) fetched without version pinning or hash verification, distributed from the same operator-controlled object store.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
